pattern: CMOS logic inverter: complementary NMOS+PMOS sharing gate (in) and drain (out), PMOS to the high rail, NMOS to gnd
note: no tanh. The (hysteretic) input PROGRAMS the pull-up/pull-down conductances -- Rup low when in low, Rdown low when in high -- each with a leakage floor 1/rleak that stays on, so the off-path draws the static current (power match). A small positive feedback h from the output shifts the threshold with state (light hysteresis) to sharpen switching. Output = the resulting divider into the load C: linear-algebraic, cheap per step, linear between input changes so the solver strides. NAND/NOR = series/parallel conductance programming.
